meaning: adj.大量的,群集的,多种多样的
examples: Multitudinous of the back, in stop porphyrin Wo mind wandering.
There are multitudinous layers of the Abyss, perhaps even an infinite number.
I've heard enough, by now, to catalog the multitudinous complaints into several broad categories.
The multitudinous on-site restaurant offerings have been augmented with the sleek steak house Prime 44 West.
Lobbyists swarm Capitol Hill, the White House, and the multitudinous federal bureaucracies throughout Washington.
